conditional_note: CALCCELLFOCUS / CALCDBFOCUS resolve to StyleSettings::GetAccentColor() (-> the accentColor=@primary slot) only as the DEFAULT colour. ColorConfig_Impl::GetColorValue returns the user's stored Tools>Options>Application Colors value first and falls back to this default only when that stored value is COL_AUTO ('Automatic'). A non-default profile bypasses the Material accent entirely. Source-confirmed; the routing is NOT unconditional and this contract pins only that the default-colour source chain is unbroken.
token_pairing_note: The wired header-selection fill is @primary (from CALCCELLFOCUS -> GetAccentColor -> accentColor slot), while the selected label text is @on-primary-container (GetHighlightTextColor -> highlightTextColor slot). This @primary fill + @on-primary-container text pairing differs from docs/design/10-writer-calc.md 10.3's implication that the fill resolves through highlightColor/@primary-container; the contract pins what the code does, and the pairing's contrast is a build-bound V-gate follow-up (not claimed here).

style_slots.role: The default colour CALCCELLFOCUS/CALCDBFOCUS resolve to; paints the active-cell cursor ring, the DB/AutoFilter range highlight and the selected-header fill.
The generic StyleSettings selection wash (consumed by other surfaces; pinned here for token fidelity).
The bold/selected header label text colour (hdrcont.cxx GetHighlightTextColor()).
The idle header face fill (hdrcont.cxx GetFaceColor()).

colorcfg_bridge.note: ColorConfig::GetDefaultColor special-cases CALCCELLFOCUS and CALCDBFOCUS to the accent slot; both contiguous case->GetAccentColor statements are pinned so a rewire away from the Material accent fails closed.

header_highlight.note: ScHeaderControl::DoPaint reads the idle face (GetFaceColor), the idle label text (GetButtonTextColor), the bold/selected label text (GetHighlightTextColor), the rule line (GetShadowColor) and the selected-header fill (GetColorValue(CALCCELLFOCUS)).

cell_cursor.note: The active-cell cursor ring (UpdateCursorOverlay), the DB/AutoFilter range highlight (UpdateSelectionOverlay), the autofill handle and the content highlight all read CALCCELLFOCUS/CALCDBFOCUS through ScModule::GetColorConfig().

density.note: Compact/comfortable column-width and row-height defaults have zero native selection code anywhere in sc/ or vcl/ (grep-confirmed empty). Specified in the design chapter, not implemented; must never be promoted to an implemented claim without a real density code path plus a build.

alignment.note: Numeric-right / text-left alignment is pre-existing stock ScPatternAttr/output.cxx behaviour that predates and is orthogonal to the Material redesign; nothing theme-related is wired, so there is no Material consumption to pin.

gridlines.note: CALCGRID is consumed for gridlines in gridwin4.cxx but is NOT special-cased in ColorConfig::GetDefaultColor (no 'case CALCGRID:'), so it falls through to the fixed cAutoColors default (COL_GRAY3 light / COL_GRAY7 dark), NOT any definition.xml token. This contradicts the design chapter's 'stroke-thin @outline-variant gridlines' claim, so the status is 'divergent'. FAIL-CLOSED DUAL-UPDATE: if a future commit adds a 'case CALCGRID:' Material routing (a real, build-verifiable fix), the absent_marker assertion fails closed and this status MUST be flipped in the SAME change, or the checker blocks its own intended improvement.

notes: Pins the source chain from the definition.xml accent/highlight/face slots through ColorConfig::GetDefaultColor's CALCCELLFOCUS/CALCDBFOCUS accent routing to the real sc header/grid paint call sites. Source evidence only: runtime_verified is false; no native build, grid pixels, contrast, or interaction is claimed. Three carve-outs (density, alignment, gridlines) keep an honest non-implemented status so none can be silently promoted; gridlines is a real, currently-true divergence from the design chapter, not a passing claim.
